quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 8 Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: Defining and Using SOH CAH TOA
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: In a right triangle, the sine of an acute angle is defined as:
options: The side opposite the angle divided by the hypotenuse
The side adjacent to the angle divided by the hypotenuse
The side opposite the angle divided by the side adjacent to the angle
The hypotenuse divided by the side opposite the angle
questions.explanation: SOH: Sine = Opposite / Hypotenuse. The sine ratio compares the side directly across from the angle to the hypotenuse of the right triangle.

questions.question: In a right triangle, the cosine of an acute angle is defined as:
options: The side adjacent to the angle divided by the hypotenuse
The side opposite the angle divided by the hypotenuse
The side opposite the angle divided by the side adjacent to the angle
The hypotenuse divided by the side adjacent to the angle
questions.explanation: CAH: Cosine = Adjacent / Hypotenuse. The cosine ratio compares the side next to the angle (not the hypotenuse) to the hypotenuse.

questions.question: In a right triangle, the tangent of an acute angle is defined as:
options: The side opposite the angle divided by the side adjacent to the angle
The side adjacent to the angle divided by the hypotenuse
The side opposite the angle divided by the hypotenuse
The hypotenuse divided by the side adjacent to the angle
questions.explanation: TOA: Tangent = Opposite / Adjacent. The tangent ratio compares the side directly across from the angle to the side next to the angle.

questions.question: In right $\triangle ABC$ with the right angle at $C$, the hypotenuse is $\overline{AB}$. What is $\sin A$?
questions.explanation: SOH: Sine = Opposite / Hypotenuse. From angle $A$, the opposite side is $\overline{BC}$ and the hypotenuse is $\overline{AB}$. So $\sin A = \frac{BC}{AB}$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle ABC$ with the right angle at $C$, the hypotenuse is $\overline{AB}$. What is $\cos A$?
questions.explanation: CAH: Cosine = Adjacent / Hypotenuse. From angle $A$, the adjacent side is $\overline{AC}$ and the hypotenuse is $\overline{AB}$. So $\cos A = \frac{AC}{AB}$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle ABC$ with the right angle at $C$, the hypotenuse is $\overline{AB}$. What is $\tan A$?
questions.explanation: TOA: Tangent = Opposite / Adjacent. From angle $A$, the opposite side is $\overline{BC}$ and the adjacent side is $\overline{AC}$. So $\tan A = \frac{BC}{AC}$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle PQR$ with the right angle at $R$, $PQ = 10$, $QR = 6$, and $PR = 8$. What is $\sin P$?
questions.explanation: From angle $P$, the opposite side is $\overline{QR} = 6$ and the hypotenuse is $\overline{PQ} = 10$. So $\sin P = \frac{6}{10} = \frac{3}{5}$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle PQR$ with the right angle at $R$, $PQ = 10$, $QR = 6$, and $PR = 8$. What is $\cos P$?
questions.explanation: From angle $P$, the adjacent side is $\overline{PR} = 8$ and the hypotenuse is $\overline{PQ} = 10$. So $\cos P = \frac{8}{10} = \frac{4}{5}$.

questions.question: In right $\triangle PQR$ with the right angle at $R$, $PQ = 10$, $QR = 6$, and $PR = 8$. What is $\tan P$?
questions.explanation: From angle $P$, the opposite side is $\overline{QR} = 6$ and the adjacent side is $\overline{PR} = 8$. So $\tan P = \frac{6}{8} = \frac{3}{4}$.

questions.question: Which memory phrase helps recall the three basic trigonometric ratios?
questions.explanation: SOH CAH TOA: Sine = Opposite/Hypotenuse, Cosine = Adjacent/Hypotenuse, Tangent = Opposite/Adjacent. This phrase helps remember which sides to use for each ratio.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the three trigonometric ratios — sine, cosine, and tangent. You can correctly identify opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse from any acute angle.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Remember SOH CAH TOA: Sine = Opposite/Hypotenuse, Cosine = Adjacent/Hypotenuse, Tangent = Opposite/Adjacent. Always identify the hypotenuse first, then locate the opposite and adjacent sides relative to the angle.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: (1) the hypotenuse is always the longest side and is opposite the right angle, (2) the opposite side is across from the angle you're using, (3) the adjacent side is next to the angle but is not the hypotenuse.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! SOH CAH TOA is the key: Sine uses Opposite and Hypotenuse, Cosine uses Adjacent and Hypotenuse, Tangent uses Opposite and Adjacent. Always start by identifying which side is which relative to the given angle.
